title: Error vs Exception
description: When to use which? Best practices.

slides.title: Errors vs Exceptions
slides.content:
# When to Use What?

*   **Errors**: Represent unrecoverable problems or programming mistakes (like calling undefined function). In PHP 7+, many errors are throwable. Usually you don't catch them unless you have a good reason.
*   **Exceptions**: Represent exceptional but recoverable conditions in your application logic (e.g., validation failure, file not found). Use exceptions to handle these gracefully.

Best practice: Use exceptions for domain-specific errors. Let system errors (like parse errors, type errors) usually go uncaught, but you can catch them at the top level to log and show a user-friendly message.

slides.title: Error Handling Strategy

quizData.question: Should you catch every error in your application?

options.text: No, let some errors bubble up to the global handler

quizData.explanation: It's best to catch expected exceptions and have a global handler for unexpected ones, rather than catching everything indiscriminately.
